add_node_subtitle: Register a new node to your account.
add_node_limit: Free tier allows 3 nodes. Upgrade for more.
landing_hero_sub1: Type "why is pve-storage slow?" and get a plain-English answer.
landing_steps_sub: From zero to full homelab intelligence in one terminal session.
landing_step1_desc: One command installs a lightweight agent on each node. No inbound ports to open, under 50MB RAM at runtime.
landing_step2_desc: CPU, memory, disk, network, Docker containers, services. Outbound only — your firewall stays closed.
landing_step3_desc: Type questions in plain English. Get smart alerts, metric diagnostics, and daily intelligence digests with per-node grades.
landing_features_sub: labwatch understands your homelab, not just its metrics.
landing_feat_nlq_desc: Type "why is pve-storage slow?" and get a diagnostic answer. Not a graph — an explanation with metric context.
landing_feat_alerts_desc: Deduplication, auto-resolution, and threshold monitoring. Catches slow disk fills and resource pressure before they become outages.
landing_feat_fleet_desc: Daily plain-English digest of your entire lab. Per-node letter grades, trends, anomalies, and actionable recommendations.
landing_feat_docker_desc: Container health, restart loop detection, CPU and memory per container. Sees inside your stacks, not just the host.
landing_feat_stack_desc: Runs alongside Grafana, Uptime Kuma, Prometheus. labwatch adds the intelligence layer they don't have.
landing_feat_oss_desc: MIT-licensed Go agent you can audit. Outbound-only connections. Self-host the server or use our hosted version. Your data stays yours.
landing_demo_sub: Real queries, real answers. No graphs to interpret.
landing_install_sub: Works on Debian, Ubuntu, Fedora, Arch, Alpine, and NixOS.
landing_install_note: Or download the binary directly from
landing_install_note2: Open source — AGPL-3.0 server, MIT agent.
landing_pricing_sub: Start free. Scale when you need to.
landing_price_free_desc: Everything, capped at 3 nodes. No credit card.
landing_price_starter_desc: For small homelabs with a handful of nodes.
landing_price_pro_desc: For serious labs — unlimited nodes and priority.
landing_price_selfhost_desc: Run the server on your own hardware. Full control.
login_subtitle: Sign in with your email or API token.
login_token_hint: This is the 64-character token you received when you signed up. You can also find it in /etc/labwatch/config.yaml on your node.
setpw_subtitle: Set a password for your account so you can log in with email.
signup_subtitle: Free tier — no credit card required.
signup_email_hint: We'll send your credentials here. No spam.
signup_password_hint: Used to log in to your dashboard. You can also log in with your API token.
signup_hostname_hint: Display name for your first node. You can rename it later.
signup_free_note: Free tier includes 3 nodes, 30-day metric retention, and basic alerts.
signup_step4_note: Metrics will appear within 60 seconds of starting the agent.
dashboard_upgrade_checkout_failed: Could not start checkout. Please try again in a moment.
dashboard_widget_library_subtitle: add more widgets to your dashboard
dashboard_demo_banner: This is a demo with sample data.
dashboard_ask_demo_placeholder: Try asking about the demo lab...
dashboard_no_labs_desc: Install the agent on any Linux machine to start collecting metrics and intelligence.
dashboard_query_cpu: which node uses the most cpu?
dashboard_query_disk: how much disk space do I have?
dashboard_widget_desc_fleet_summary: total nodes, alerts, uptime at a glance
dashboard_widget_desc_alerts: latest fired alerts across your fleet
dashboard_widget_desc_alert_rollup: top labs by active alert count
dashboard_widget_desc_disk_pressure: labs closest to running out of disk
lab_admin_delete_desc: Permanently remove this lab and all its data. This cannot be undone.
lab_admin_delete_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete this lab? This cannot be undone.
support_subtitle: Need help with labwatch? We're here for you.
support_email_desc: Send us an email and we'll get back to you within 24 hours.
support_docs_desc: Browse our docs for setup guides, troubleshooting, and API reference.
support_github_desc: Found a bug or have a feature request? Open an issue on GitHub.
support_faq_q1: How do I find my API token?
support_faq_a1: Your API token is in /etc/labwatch/config.yaml on any node where the agent is installed. You can also find it in your dashboard under Administration.
support_faq_q2: The agent isn't sending metrics. What do I do?
support_faq_a2: Check that the agent is running with 'systemctl status labwatch'. Verify your config.yaml has the correct api_endpoint and token. Check firewall rules allow outbound HTTPS.
support_faq_q3: Can I self-host the labwatch server?
support_faq_a3: Yes! Self-hosting is a first-class path. See /self-hosted for the full docker-compose quickstart, environment variables, and agent config — the server is AGPL-3.0 and the agent is MIT licensed on GitHub.
dashboard_edit_hint: drag to reorder · × to remove · + add widget at bottom
landing_price_business_desc: For teams running 50+ servers who need SLA-backed support and custom integrations.
about_what_p1: labwatch is a lightweight monitoring tool designed specifically for homelab infrastructure. A single Go binary agent runs on each node and reports CPU, memory, disk, network, and container metrics to a central server. The dashboard gives you a real-time view of your entire lab from one place.
about_what_p2: Unlike enterprise monitoring platforms that require extensive setup and configuration, labwatch is designed to work out of the box. Install the agent, point it at your server, and you have monitoring in under a minute.
about_who_p1: Anyone running a homelab. Whether you have a single Raspberry Pi or a full Proxmox cluster with a dozen nodes, labwatch scales to your setup. It is particularly useful for:
about_who_b1: Proxmox and hypervisor operators who want per-node and per-container visibility
about_who_b2: Docker self-hosters who want to track container health alongside host metrics
about_who_b3: Anyone who wants a simpler alternative to Prometheus + Grafana for small deployments
about_stack_desc: labwatch is built with simplicity in mind. No external databases, no message queues, no container orchestration required.
about_oss_p1: labwatch is open source. The server is licensed under AGPL-3.0 and the agent under MIT. You can self-host the entire stack or use the hosted version at labwatch.dev.
billing_success_heading: Thanks — your upgrade is being processed
billing_success_desc: Stripe has confirmed your payment. Your plan will be upgraded within a few seconds once the webhook lands. If you don't see the new limits on your dashboard, refresh in a moment.
billing_cancel_desc: No charge was made. You can pick up where you left off any time from your dashboard, or check the pricing page if you want to compare plans.
lab_alert_thresholds_desc: Customize when alerts trigger for this node. Values are percentages.
lab_public_status_desc: Share a read-only status page for this node with anyone.
lab_maintenance_desc: Silence all alerts for this lab during planned work.
alerts_subtitle: Describe the alert you want in plain English. We'll parse it, show you the generated rule, and apply it when you confirm.
alerts_placeholder: e.g. alert me when any lab's disk fills past 85%
alerts_chip_cpu: alert me when CPU goes above 90%
alerts_chip_disk: warn me when disk fills past 85%
alerts_chip_gpu: alert me if GPU temperature exceeds 80C
alerts_chip_load: notify me when load average is above 10
alerts_hint: Green = customized by you. Applying a rule updates the matching threshold here.
notif_subtitle: Add a channel to get notified when something breaks in your lab. Supports ntfy, Discord, Slack, Telegram, Gotify, Pushover, Apprise, and generic webhooks.
notif_add_hint: Pick a provider, fill in the details, click Save & test.
notif_no_channels: No channels yet. Add one below.
signup_step1_title: paste this on any linux box — installs + starts the agent
signup_copy_hint: ↑ click anywhere on the command to copy
signup_link_expires: link expires in 15 minutes — if it does, come back and we’ll regenerate.
add_node_onecommand_desc: Downloads the agent, writes config.yaml with your credentials, and starts the service. No token pasting.
dashboard_search_placeholder: Filter labs by hostname or OS…  ( press / )
